assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | 1 test function removed (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers), 1 added as replacement (test_recommend_purls_dedup), 1 modified (test_recommend_purls_basic), 3 new tests in new file; structural signals are both additive and reductive |'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Test Change Analysis' section: 'test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers: Removed (qualifier behavior no longer exists) (REMOVED)'. Also in the Classification paragraph: 'The removal of test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers is a reductive signal (coverage of qualifier-specific behavior removed).'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md describes test_recommend_purls_basic as 'Assertions tightened to verify simplified PURLs without qualifiers (MODIFIED)' — it characterizes the change as 'tightened' rather than noting the shift from a fully qualified PURL assertion to a versioned PURL without qualifiers. The report does not characterize the assertion change as a relaxation or note that it contributes to the MIXED classification. The criterion-1.md file shows the new assertion 'assert_eq!(body.items[0].purl, "pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12")' but does not discuss the original assertion value or characterize the change as a relaxation.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Test Change Analysis' section lists under 'New file: tests/api/purl_simplify.rs' three added test functions: test_simplified_purl_no_version (ADDED), test_simplified_purl_mixed_types (ADDED), test_simplified_purl_ordering_preserved (ADDED). The summary table also says '3 new tests in new file'.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Test Change Analysis' section: 'test_recommend_purls_dedup: Added as replacement to verify dedup behavior (ADDED)'. The summary table row also lists '1 added as replacement (test_recommend_purls_dedup)' as part of the structural signals.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. No code files (.rs, .toml, .py, .js, .ts, etc.) exist in the outputs directory. A find command for code file extensions returned no results.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings with verdicts (PASS/FAIL/WARN) and detailed analysis but does not merge the PR. It ends with '*This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr*' — a review comment, not a merge action. No merge commands or merge API calls appear in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: Summary table (report.md line 14): '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | ...' with inline details. Detailed 'Test Change Analysis' section follows (lines 37-49) with per-file breakdowns of MODIFIED/REMOVED/ADDED functions and a 'Classification: MIXED' paragraph explaining both structural signals ('removal of test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers is a reductive signal') and semantic assessment ('The semantic assessment confirms that the removed test's coverage was invalidated by the production change').

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The Test Change Analysis section in report.md references specific test file content: function names (test_recommend_purls_basic, test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers, test_recommend_purls_dedup), their status (MODIFIED, REMOVED, ADDED), and file paths (tests/api/purl_recommend.rs, tests/api/purl_simplify.rs). The classification paragraph discusses 'coverage of qualifier-specific behavior removed' and 'replaced by test_recommend_purls_dedup which tests the new dedup behavior' — these reference test file content and functions, not acceptance criteria or task requirements.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Repetitive Test Detection: WARN (3 tests in purl_simplify.rs share same algorithm, parameterization candidates); Test Documentation: PASS (all test functions have doc comments); Eval Quality: N/A |'. Eval Quality is explicitly listed as N/A within the Test Quality row.
